message.content:
Read /Users/bhyslop/.claude/projects/-Users-bhyslop-projects-rbm-alpha-recipemuster/96b94224-276e-4289-bd7b-efa096f36200/tool-results/mcp-vvx-jjx-1778634994513.txt in chunks of ~85 lines using offset/limit until you have read all 1,125 lines. This is the output of `jjx_show {target: "BA", detail: true, remaining: true}` for the "BA" heat in Job Jockey.

Return a structured summary with the following sections, quoting verbatim where indicated:

1. **Heat header**: firemark, silks, status (racing/stabled), pace counts (total/open/closed/remaining), any progress indicator. Quote verbatim.

2. **Paddock** (if shown in this output — note that paddock content is normally in gazette_out.md, not the show output, but mention if it appears): one-line summary of heat shape/goal.

3. **Recent work** (last 3-5 closed paces): coronet, silks, one-line summary each.

4. **Remaining paces** (open paces in order — this is the critical part for grooming): for EACH open pace, list:
   - Full coronet (e.g. `₢BAAAk`) and silks
   - One-sentence summary of the docket goal
   - Approximate docket size (small/medium/large)

5. **Notable structural observations**: any patterns you notice — clusters of related paces, paces that look stale or out-of-order, paces with very thin or very thick dockets, dependency oddities (paces that seem to belong elsewhere in the order).

Be thorough — the user is grooming this heat and needs a complete picture of what's left, with full coronets always shown (never abbreviate to the last 3 chars). Do not paraphrase coronets or silks — copy them exactly.

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read the file in chunks to get the full picture of the BA heat.

content.content:
1	Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 85%, 228 GB: 85%
2	Heat: rbk-11-mvp-parallel-cleanups (₣BA)
3	Status: racing
4	Created: 260426
5	
6	
7	## Paces
8	
9	### jettison-multi-platform-cascade-fix (₢BAAAG) [rough]
10	
11	## Character
12	
13	Targeted machinery fix on a defect surfaced empirically by ₣A_'s AAS pace.
14	Small-diff, high-confidence change once the cascade traversal is written —
15	the OCI Distribution Spec already nails down the index-to-children
16	relationship, and `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact` already implements the
17	read-side of that traversal. This pace mirrors that traversal on the
18	delete side.
19	
20	The risk surface is narrow: existing single-platform jettisons (pouch on
21	bind, anything else not pushed via buildx) must remain unchanged. The new
22	code path activates only when the manifest at the locator is an OCI image
23	index / Docker manifest list.
24	
25	Verification is concrete: the batch_vouch fixture (already implemented under
26	AAS) is the regression test. It currently FAILS at step 03 because vouch
27	jettison leaves the package container in GAR's ListPackages; after this pace
28	lands, it must pass step 03 (tally pending) through step 06 (abjure) cleanly.
29	
30	## Docket
31	
32	### Read-only investigation completed (carryover from prior chat)
33	
34	A previous mounting did cadence-step-2 (the read pass) and pre-validated the
35	docket against the actual code. The premise holds: all three reference points
36	(`zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`'s mediaType branch, `rbfl_jettison`'s
37	single-DELETE shape, `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh`'s buildx push) match
38	the docket's description structurally. No design pivots were surfaced.
39	
40	Three small decisions were ratified by the working chat:
41	
42	1. **Helper extraction is in.** Add `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` to
43	   `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` next to `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`. Args:
44	   `(token, package, tag)`. Side effects: per-digest DELETEs only — the
45	   index-by-tag DELETE stays in `rbfl_jettison` so the existing single-DELETE
46	   path is the unconditional finalizer. Mirrors the read-side helper's
47	   curl+jq pattern.
48	
49	2. **Retrofit existing DELETE's stderr capture in the same notch.** The
50	   current `rbfl_jettison` curl doesn't redirect stderr; BCG's "Stderr
51	   Capture — Never Suppress" applies. Single-line addition (`2>"${z_stderr_file}"`
52	   plus a die-message reference). No behavior change.
53	
54	3. **RBSIJ amendment rides the same notch.** The spec is small and ships
55	   with the behavior. Two new operation steps between current "Confirm
56	   Jettison" and "Jettison Tag":
57	   - **Probe Manifest Type** — GET manifest, branch 404 (early-exit
58	     idempotent success) vs 200 (continue), extract mediaType.
59	   - **Cascade Children if Index** — when mediaType matches
60	     `*manifest.list*` or `*image.index*`, enumerate `.manifests[].digest`
61	     and DELETE each child by digest before the existing tag DELETE.
62	   Plus a NOTE explaining the producer-side asymmetry (single-platform
63	   `docker push` vs multi-platform `docker buildx --push`).
64	
65	### Empirical motivation (recorded for the next mounting)
66	
67	`rbfl_jettison` calls Docker Registry V2 `DELETE /v2/<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`.
68	For single-platform pushes (e.g. pouch, which is `docker push` from
69	`rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`), this removes the only manifest, the package
70	becomes empty, and GAR drops it from `ListPackages`. For multi-platform
71	buildx pushes (vouch in `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh`, all reliquary tool
72	images, all enshrinements), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing
73	N per-platform manifests; DELETE-by-tag removes only the index, leaving the
74	N children as untagged content. The package container persists in
75	`ListPackages` because it still has manifests.
76	
77	`zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` and consequently `rbfl_tally` and `rbfl_rekon`
78	enumerate via `ListPackages`, so they continue to report the basename present
79	after a jettison-by-tag against multi-platform content. AAS's batch_vouch
80	fixture exposed this as a tally-still-vouched failure post-jettison; the
81	morning's stale-hallmark abjure (which uses the GAR REST package-delete path,
82	not the V2 manifest path) cleanly removed all six packages including vouch
83	— confirming the package container persistence is real and is exactly what
84	abjure already overcomes.
85

86	The asymmetry was bind_lifecycle's pouch jettison passing while batch_vouch's
87	vouch jettison failed; same locator grammar, different push mechanism on the
88	producer side.
89	
90	### Approach: cascade through the index
91	
92	In `rbfl_jettison`, after parsing the locator and authenticating:
93	
94	1. **Probe** — GET the manifest at `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>` with the
95	   manifest Accept header (use `ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES`). Capture
96	   status, response, stderr to per-call temp files under
97	   `${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}probe_*`.
98	2. **Branch on probe status:**
99	   - `404` → preserve existing idempotent semantics: emit
100	     `"Jettisoned or nonexistent: <locator>"` and return success.
101	   - `200` → fall through.
102	   - other → die with status code.
103	3. **Read mediaType** with `jq -r '.mediaType // empty' ... 2>/dev/null || true`
104	   (matching `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`'s pattern). Empty mediaType is
105	   safe — falls through to single-DELETE behavior.
106	4. **Conditional cascade** — `case "${z_media_type}" in
107	   *manifest.list*|*image.index*) zrbfc_jettison_index_children ... ;; esac`.
108	5. **DELETE-by-tag** (existing code, unchanged behavior) — remains as the
109	   unconditional final step. Accepts 202|204.
110	
111	The cascade helper itself: GET the index manifest (manifest Accept header),
112	extract `.manifests[].digest` to a temp file, load-then-iterate (BCG
113	stdin-consumption discipline), per-child DELETE with 202|204 acceptance.
114	
115	### Surface
116	
117	- **`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`** — extend `rbfl_jettison` with
118	  probe + branch + cascade-call before the existing DELETE. Retrofit
119	  stderr capture on the existing DELETE.
120	- **`Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`** — new helper
121	  `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` near `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`.
122	  Reuses existing constants: `ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE`,
123	  `ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES`, `RBCC_CURL_*`.
124	- **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc`** — two new operation
125	  steps + a NOTE about the producer-side asymmetry.
126	
127	No tabtarget surface change. No new prefixes. No new modules. No new
128	constants in regimes.
129	
130	### BCG patterns to preserve
131	
132	Working chat noted these explicitly so they survive the next mounting:
133	
134	- All new curl calls capture stderr to per-call temp files and reference
135	  them in `buc_die` messages.
136	- `local -r` for one-shot locals; plain `local` only for per-iteration loop
137	  variables (BCG Exception 2).
138	- Load-then-iterate for the child-digest loop (read digests into array,
139	  iterate over `${!arr[@]}` with index discriminator) — protects against
140	  stdin consumption hazards.
141	- `case "${z_media_type}" in *manifest.list*|*image.index*) ... ;; esac`
142	  for pattern matching, not `[[ ]]`.
143	- HTTP code branches via `case`, accepting 202|204 for DELETEs and 200
144	  (with 404 idempotent) for the probe GET.
145	
146	Important `rbgu_http_json` caveat: it accepts only 200/201/204 in
147	`rbgu_http_require_ok`. The V2 manifest DELETE path returns 202, so the
148	existing single-DELETE pattern (raw curl with explicit `-w "%{http_code}"`
149	plus a case-statement check) is the right model — do not refactor those
150	calls onto `rbgu_http_json`.
151	
152	### Verification
153	
154	1. **Regression — batch_vouch fixture must pass end-to-end:**
155	   `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.batch-vouch.sh rbtdrc_batch_vouch_lifecycle`
156	   (currently fails at step 03 tally pending; after this pace must pass
157	   through step 07 passed).
158	2. **Bind/pouch unaffected:** `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.four-mode.sh
159	   rbtdrc_fourmode_bind_lifecycle` — single-platform jettison path must
160	   still pass.
161	3. **All four single-mode SingleCases GREEN** — sanity sweep:
162	   conjure_lifecycle, bind_lifecycle, graft_lifecycle, kludge_lifecycle.
163	4. **Fast suite GREEN** — no regression on non-crucible tests
164	   (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`).
165	5. **Manual one-shot:** ordain a fresh hallmark, jettison vouch via
166	   `rbw-iJh.DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage.sh`, run `rbw-ft` —
167	   the hallmark should reflect "pending" (image+about, no vouch) rather
168	   than "vouched" with all six basenames.
169	6. **Cost discipline:** the regression test is one ordain (≈3-5min Cloud
170	   Build) per fixture run. Don't loop unnecessarily.

171	
172	### Hard dependency notes
173	
174	- `₢BAAAF` (imageops-fixtures) **depends on this pace landing**. BAAAF's
175	  `muster_absent` cases assert concrete absence, which requires the cascade
176	  fix to make packages truly empty.
177	- This pace is independent of `₢BAAAA` (oauth hygiene), `₢BAAAC` (OCI URL
178	  keyword extraction), and `₢BAAAB` (BCG shellcheck sweep) — disjoint
179	  surfaces.
180	- **Test-suite reservation:** ₣BA's paddock blocks crucible/service suites
181	  while ₣A_ is racing on live infra. The notch criteria (bind_lifecycle +
182	  batch_vouch GREEN) require coordination — either wait for ₣A_'s wrap, or
183	  the user authorizes a partial-verification notch (bash -n + shellcheck +
184	  fast suite) with full crucible verification deferred. Mounting agent
185	  should surface this before running fixtures.
186	
187	### Out of scope
188	
189	- **Changing `rbfl_abjure`.** Abjure already uses GAR REST package-delete
190	  and works correctly for multi-platform content; don't refactor it.
191	- **Changing `zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` semantics.** Package-level
192	  enumeration is correct given the post-cascade behavior. Don't switch to
193	  tag-level enumeration.
194	- **Adding new tabtargets.** The existing `rbw-iJh` /
195	  `DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage` (and its sibling `rbw-iJr` / `rbw-iJe`
196	  introduced by BAAAD) suffice; the cascade is a behavior change inside
197	  `rbfl_jettison`, not a new operation.
198	- **Changing the producer side** (rbgjv03's buildx push, rbgjb*'s buildx
199	  pushes, enshrine's skopeo copy). Multi-platform pushes are a feature, not
200	  a defect; the consumer side (jettison) just needs to handle them.
201	- **Cascading abjure semantics into jettison** (i.e., letting jettison
202	  delete a whole package, not a tag). The spec for jettison is "by locator"
203	  not "by package"; broadening would conflate operations.
204	- **Backward-compat shims** for the silent-no-op behavior. Hard cutover:
205	  after this pace, jettison-by-tag against a multi-platform locator does
206	  the right thing; nothing should depend on the broken behavior.
207	- **Refactoring the existing single DELETE onto `rbgu_http_json`.** That
208	  helper rejects 202; the V2 manifest DELETE path returns 202. Keep the
209	  raw curl + explicit code check.
210	
211	### Cadence
212	
213	1. Mount BA, parade this pace, read this docket.
214	2. Read in order before any edit:
215	   - `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` — `rbfl_jettison` and the existing DELETE
216	     pattern
217	   - `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` — `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact` for the mediaType
218	     branch shape and the curl+jq pattern
219	   - `rbgu_Utility.sh` — `rbgu_http_json` and acceptance helpers (note
220	     200/201/204-only acceptance — see caveat above)
221	   - `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` — confirm buildx push shape
222	3. Implement the cascade. Single notch when:
223	   - bash -n / shellcheck clean on touched files
224	   - bind_lifecycle GREEN (no regression)
225	   - batch_vouch GREEN (the regression test passes)
226	4. Wrap.
227	
228	### References
229	
230	- ₣A_'s AAS docket and wrap summary — full empirical investigation and
231	  decision history.
232	- `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` — `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact` is the
233	  read-side mediaType branch, the structural template for the delete-side
234	  cascade. Insert `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` adjacent.
235	- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` — `rbfl_jettison` is the function to
236	  extend.
237	- `Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh` — `rbgu_http_json` /
238	  `rbgu_http_require_ok` reference for HTTP helper acceptance behavior.
239	- `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` — confirms vouch is a
240	  multi-platform buildx push (canonical example).
241	- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc` — spec to amend.
242	- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` — BCG patterns: stderr
243	  capture, load-then-iterate, local -r discipline.
244	
245	### oauth-trust-hygiene-sweep (₢BAAAA) [rough]
246	
247	Drafted from ₢A_AAN in ₣A_.
248	
249	## Character
250	
251	Mechanical apply. All design decisions are baked into this docket; the agent's job is to execute the listed edits, run the listed tests, and notch with the listed file list. No options to evaluate. No "decide between..." sections. If anything looks ambiguous, the docket is the source of truth — re-read it rather than improvise.
252	
253	This docket was built and reviewed in a prior chat; its line numbers and diffs were verified against the working tree at slate time. Spot-check before applying — line numbers may drift if other officia touch the target files.
254	
255	## Pre-flight (re-verify before edits)

256	
257	Run these to establish a known baseline:
258	
259	```
260	git status --short                           # expect clean of AAN target files (see file list at end)
261	tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh                  # expect 93/93 green (47+23+8+15)
262	shellcheck Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh Tools/buk/buh_handbook.sh \
263	           Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh \
264	           Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh Tools/rbk/rbrp_regime.sh 2>&1 | grep -c '^In '
265	# expect ~119 warnings baseline (post-AAN target: ~110, drops 9: 1 SC2034 from F1, 7 SC2034 from F6a, 1-2 SC2153 from F6b directives)
266	```
267	
268	If baseline differs significantly, investigate before editing. Another officium may have touched these files.
269	
270	## Cross-officium constraints (HARD)
271	
272	A parallel officium may be working on ₢A_AAS (theurge Rust + tabtargets). **Never** touch:
273	
274	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
275	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
276	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`
277	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/tests/rbtdtm_manifest.rs`
278	- `tt/rbtd-r.Run.batch-vouch.sh`
279	- `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.batch-vouch.sh`
280	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/scripts/rbte_engine.sh`
281	
282	Never run `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.crucible.sh` or `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.complete.sh`. Fast suite is read-only and safe.
283	
284	If `git status --short` shows uncommitted changes to AAS-affiliated files at any point, leave them alone — that's the other officium's in-flight work.
285	
286	## Apply order (one notch at the end, not per-finding)
287	
288	### Step 1 — Pure deletions (F1, F6a) and shellcheck directives (F6b)
289	
290	**F1** — Delete dead `ZRBGO_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE`. File: `Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh`, line 50:
291	
292	```bash
293	  readonly ZRBGO_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_private_key.pem"
294	```
295	
296	Delete the entire line.
297	
298	**F6a** — Delete 7 dead `ZRBGP_INFIX_*` constants in `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`. Verified zero consumers tree-wide. Lines 46, 47, 48, 53, 54, 61, 63:
299	
300	```bash
301	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
302	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
303	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
304	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
305	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
306	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_VERIFY_SA="gov_verify_sa"
307	  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_IAM="gov_iam"
308	```
309	
310	Delete those exact lines; leave the other 11 INFIX constants intact.
311	
312	**F6b** — Add file-scoped shellcheck directive to silence SC2153 false positive on cross-module `ZRBGU_PREFIX`. Apply to BOTH files (the extension to `rbgg_Governor.sh` is a confirmed freebie since both have the same false-positive shape and the file is touched by F13 anyway).
313	
314	In `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`, insert after line 25 (after `ZRBGP_SOURCED=1`):
315	
316	```bash
317	# shellcheck disable=SC2153
318	# ZRBGU_PREFIX and ZRBGU_POSTFIX_* are defined in rbgu_Utility.sh and shared
319	# across modules via the kindle/sentinel chain (zrbgu_sentinel asserted in
320	# zrbgp_kindle). Shellcheck cannot follow the runtime sourcing graph.
321	```
322	
323	In `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh`, insert the same block after the file's own `ZRBGG_SOURCED=1` line (find via grep — the multiple-inclusion guard).
324	
325	Note: the BCG-wide answer to this class of false positive is being studied under ₢A_AAY (`bcg-shellcheck-cross-module-discipline`). When AAY lands, these per-file directives become redundant and should be removed by AAY's apply pass.
326	
327	### Step 2 — F3 OAuth URL constants
328	
329	`RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL` already exists at `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh:69`. Mint two more.
330	
331	In `rbgc_Constants.sh`, after line 69 (`readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL=...`), insert:
332	
333	```bash
334	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
335	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL="https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo"
336	```
337	
338	Then four substitutions in `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`:
339	
340	- Line ~102: `"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"` → `"${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"`

341	- Line ~443: replace ONLY the bare URL portion, NOT the query string. The full line is:
342	  ```
343	  local -r z_auth_url="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?client_id=${z_client_id}&redirect_uri=urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob&scope=openid%20email%20https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform%20https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-billing&response_type=code&access_type=offline"
344	  ```
345	  Replace `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth` (the prefix) with `${RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL}` — keep the entire `?client_id=...` query string inline (it's parameterized, not extractable).
346	- Line ~485: `"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"` → `"${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"`
347	- Line ~538: `"https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo"` → `"${RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL}"`
348	
349	### Step 3 — Behavior-adjacent (F2, F5)
350	
351	**F2** — Close TOCTOU on RBRO write. `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh:504-509`. Current:
352	
353	```bash
354	  buc_step 'Store OAuth credentials'
355	  {
356	    echo "RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET=${z_client_secret}"
357	    echo "RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN=${z_refresh_token}"
358	  } > "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRO credentials file"
359	  chmod 600 "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "Failed to set RBRO file permissions"
360	```
361	
362	Replace with:
363	
364	```bash
365	  buc_step 'Store OAuth credentials'
366	  (
367	    umask 077
368	    {
369	      echo "RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET=${z_client_secret}"
370	      echo "RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN=${z_refresh_token}"
371	    } > "${z_rbro_file}"
372	  ) || buc_die "Failed to write RBRO credentials file"
373	  chmod 600 "${z_rbro_file}" || buc_die "Failed to set RBRO file permissions"
374	```
375	
376	The subshell isolates the umask change so the file is created at 0600 from the start. The chmod 600 stays as belt-and-suspenders.
377	
378	**F5** — Mint `buh_prompt_secret` and swap callsite.
379	
380	In `Tools/buk/buh_handbook.sh`, after the `buh_prompt` function (currently ending around line 237), insert:
381	
382	```bash
383	# buh_prompt_secret "prompt text"
384	# Like buh_prompt but suppresses terminal echo of typed/pasted input.
385	# Emits a trailing newline to stderr so subsequent output starts on a fresh line.
386	buh_prompt_secret() {
387	  zbuh_sentinel
388	  printf '%s' "${1:-}" >&2
389	  local z_input
390	  read -rs z_input
391	  printf '\n' >&2
392	  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
393	}
394	```
395	
396	Place this BEFORE the existing `buh_prompt_required` function so the prompts cluster together.

402	```
403

408	```

476	### Step 5 — F13 producer-reality docs + BCG brace freebie + consumer-comment fix
477	
478	**F13 — CORRECTION FROM ORIGINAL DOCKET:** the original docket asserted "RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY must hold '\n' escape sequences (literal backslash-n), NOT real newlines." This premise is **inverted** with respect to the actual code. The reality:
479	
480	- All three writer sites use `z_private_key=$(jq -r '.private_key' ...)`. `jq -r` unescapes JSON `\n` → real newlines.
481	- All three writer sites then use `printf '%s' "$z_private_key"`, preserving real newlines verbatim.
482	- The RBRA file therefore contains a multi-line `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----<real newline>...-----END-----<real newline>"`.
483	- Consumer at `rbgo_OAuth.sh:125` does `printf '%b' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}\n"`. `%b` on real newlines is a no-op; PEM has no backslashes for `%b` to process. The `%b` is harmless redundancy.
484	- The consumer comment at `rbgo_OAuth.sh:82-83` ("contains \n sequences that must become real newlines") is **misleading or stale**.
485	
486	Decision: **document reality, not the docket's misframing**. Three writer sites confirmed (rgbs_ServiceAccounts.sh checked, does NOT touch RBRA — only three writers exist).
487	
488	Also confirmed during prep: **BCG brace inconsistency** at the writer sites. `rbgu:602` and `rbgg:264` use unbraced `"$z_private_key"`; `rbgp:1332` uses braced `"${z_private_key}"`. BCG mandates braces. Brace the two unbraced sites in the same pass — confirmed freebie.
489	
490	**Producer comment + brace fix at all three sites:**
491	
492	`Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh:602` — current:
493	
494	```bash
495	  buc_log_args 'Write RBRA file'
496	  {
497	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "$z_client_email"
498	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "$z_private_key"; printf '"\n'
499	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "$z_project_id"
500	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=%s\n'  "$z_lifetime_sec"
501	  } > "${z_rbra_path}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_path}"
502	```
503	
504	Replace with:
505	
506	```bash
507	  buc_log_args 'Write RBRA file'
508	  # CAUTION: jq -r unescapes JSON \n to real newlines, so z_private_key holds a
509	  # multi-line PEM string. printf '%s' below preserves real newlines into the
510	  # RBRA file. Consumer (rbgo_OAuth.sh:zrbgo_build_jwt_capture) tolerates either

511	  # real-newline or '\n'-escape form via printf '%b'; do not "normalize" to one
512	  # form without auditing the consumer.
513	  {
514	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "${z_client_email}"
515	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "${z_private_key}"; printf '"\n'
516	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "${z_project_id}"
517	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=%s\n'  "${z_lifetime_sec}"
518	  } > "${z_rbra_path}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_path}"
519	```
520	
521	(Note: also brace the other three printf args in this block while we're here — full BCG sweep on the 4-line block.)
